memoryArtifacts.headline: Marriage
memoryArtifacts.description:
Isabella de Beauchamp, married firstly, Sir Patrick de Chaworth and, secondly, Hugh le Despenser, 1st Earl of Winchester.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Isabella de Beauchamp於大約1249年出生在英國武斯特斯爾艾爾姆利 卡素時，她的父親William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick13歲，母親Maud FitzJohn14歲。她於1276年與Sir Patrick de Chaworth VI Lord Of Kidwelly在英國格盧斯特斯爾肯普斯福德結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1306年5月30日在她的家鄉享年58歲，埋葬在英國武斯特斯爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: Of uncertain origin. If not simply an invention, it may have been devised as a feminine equivalent of   Alan   (of which it is an anagram), or a shortened form of   Alana .  It seems to have been first used by the American film actress  Lana Turner  (  1920–1995 ), whose original name was  Julia .

nameMeanings.name: Isabella
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Isabella de Beauchamp was born about 1249, in Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, England, her father, William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick, was 13 and her mother, Maud FitzJohn, was 14. She married Sir Patrick de Chaworth VI Lord Of Kidwelly in 1276, in Kempsford, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died on 30 May 1306, in her hometown, at the age of 58, and was buried in Worcestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Isabella de Beauchamp est née environ 1249, à Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Angleterre, son père, William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick, avait 13 ans et sa mère, Maud FitzJohn, avait 14 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Patrick de Chaworth VI Lord Of Kidwelly en 1276, à Kempsford, Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 30 mai 1306, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterrée à Worcestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Isabella de Beauchamp um 1249 in Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick 13 und ihre Mutter Maud FitzJohn 14. Sie hat 1276 in Kempsford, Gloucestershire, England Sir Patrick de Chaworth VI Lord Of Kidwelly geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 30. Mai 1306 im Alter von 58 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Worcestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Isabella de Beauchamp è nata il circa 1249 nel Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, England, sua padre, William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick, aveva 13 anni e sua madre, Maud FitzJohn, aveva 14 anni. Lei sposato Sir Patrick de Chaworth VI Lord Of Kidwelly nel 1276, nel Kempsford, Gloucestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì il 30 maggio 1306, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolta a Worcestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Isabella de Beauchampが約1249年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアエルムリー カストルで生まれた時，父William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwickは13歳，母Maud FitzJohnは14歳であった。1276年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアケンプスフォードでSir Patrick de Chaworth VI Lord Of Kidwellyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1306年5月30日に故郷で58歳で死去し，イギリスウオルセステルシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmor: 영국 웨스트모를랜드 샤프 스트 메리 막그다렌'스 애브브이
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Isabella de Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1249년 영국 워스스터실르 엘리 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick(은)는 13세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud FitzJohn(은)는 14세였다. 그녀는 1276년 영국 그루스스터실르 케프스퍼드에서 Sir Patrick de Chaworth VI Lord Of Kidwelly(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1306년 5월 30일 그녀의 고향에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워스스터실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Isabella de Beauchamp nasceu aproximadamente 1249, em Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick, tinha 13 anos e sua mãe, Maud FitzJohn, tinha 14 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Patrick de Chaworth VI Lord Of Kidwelly em 1276, em Kempsford, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 30 de maio de 1306, em sua cidade natal, com 58 anos, e foi sepultada em Worcestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.St Mary Magdalene's Abbey, Shap, Westmor: ст мэри магдеилине'с эбби, шап, вестморланд, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Isabella de Beauchamp родилась около 1249 г. в местности элмли кастл, ворсестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick было 13 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud FitzJohn, 14. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Patrick de Chaworth VI Lord Of Kidwelly в 1276 г. в местности кемпсфорд, глоусестиршир, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла 30 мая 1306 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 58 лет и была похоронена в местности ворсестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Isabella de Beauchamp nació aproximadamente 1249, en Elmley Castle, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Beauchamp 9th Earl of Warwick, tenía 13 años y su mamá, Maud FitzJohn, tenía 14 años. Se casó con Sir Patrick de Chaworth VI Lord Of Kidwelly en 1276, en Kempsford, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 30 de mayo de 1306, en su pueblo, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultada en Worcestershire, Inglaterra.
